expectations.text: Item 2 is routed to a skill (repeated procedure reused across 14 repos, loaded on demand), not pasted into each repo's CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: Item 2: "**Skill** — A fixed 10-step procedure reused identically across 14 repos... authoring it once as a skill lets every repo/user invoke the same steps without duplicating it in each CLAUDE.md."

expectations.text: Item 4 is routed to an MCP server (live external data) and item 5 to a subagent, with the subagent justification citing context isolation or its own context window rather than generic 'automation'
expectations.evidence: Item 4: "**MCP server** — Looking up live Salesforce ticket records requires an authenticated connection to an external system"; Item 5: "**Subagent** — ...context-heavy and self-contained; delegating it to a subagent isolates that large read workload and returns only the summary report to the main thread."

expectations.text: Item 6 is routed to a user-level home (user CLAUDE.md or user preferences), explicitly not the shared repo CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: Item 6: "**User-level CLAUDE.md / user preferences** — ...belongs in her user-level config, which applies in every project she works in without touching any repo."

expectations.text: Item 7 is NOT stored in any always-loaded doc: the answer says code/migrations own that fact and docs should reference it, since a copied version number rots
expectations.evidence: Item 7 routed to a SessionStart hook, not a doc: "a SessionStart hook that reads the current migration state and injects it into context keeps Claude accurate without a hardcoded value in CLAUDE.md that would silently drift" — migrations own the fact and the copied-value-rots rationale is explicit.

expectations.text: Item 8 is a plain one-off prompt with nothing persisted, and no item is assigned to two mechanisms at once
expectations.evidence: Item 8: "**Plain one-off prompt** — A single rename-today task has no recurrence or reuse, so it needs no persistent mechanism". Each of items 1-8 names exactly one mechanism.
